reasoning: The informal statement asserts associativity of addition in ℤ/nℤ. The Lean statement uses `ZMod n`, Mathlib's standard encoding of ℤ/nℤ, and asserts exactly the associativity law (x + y) + z = x + (y + z) for all x, y, z : ZMod n. The quantification over all n : ℕ captures the "for any n" reading of the informal statement. Even the edge case n = 0 (where ZMod 0 = ℤ) still satisfies associativity, so no spurious restriction or extension is introduced. The types, hypotheses, and conclusion all match the informal claim.
